title: Formal Risk Assessment Evidence and Residual Risk Score Trend

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI DSS 12.3.2 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that PCI DSS 12.3.2 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.11.85: Formal Risk Assessment Evidence and Residual Risk Score Trend.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.11.85' running on index=grc sourcetype="grc:risk_register" — risk_id, pci_relevant, residual_score, review_date, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=grc sourcetype="grc:risk_register" earliest=-730d
| where pci_relevant="true"
| eval overdue=if(now()>strptime(review_date,"%Y-%m-%d"),1,0)
| stats latest(residual_score) as score, max(overdue) as past_due by risk_id, title
| where past_due=1 OR score>15
| sort score
description: Tracks annual enterprise risk assessment submissions including PCI-scoped risk items and residual scores so targeted risk analyses required by the standard are current and leadership-reviewed.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for PCI DSS compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes grc and sourcetypes grc:risk_register. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Align `review_date` to annual cycle. (2) Map `score` scale 1–25 consistently. (3) Escalate `past_due` to risk committee. (4) Export closed reviews for PCI policy pack. Indexes required: `index=grc`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=grc:risk_register`.
visualization: Line (residual_score trend in summary index), table (past_due risks), heatmap (domain x score).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk DB Connect or `sourcetype="grc:risk_register"`.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=grc` `sourcetype="grc:risk_register"` — `risk_id`, `pci_relevant`, `residual_score`, `review_date`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Align `review_date` to annual cycle. (2) Map `score` scale 1–25 consistently. (3) Escalate `past_due` to risk committee. (4) Export closed reviews for PCI policy pack.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=grc sourcetype="grc:risk_register" earliest=-730d
| where pci_relevant="true"
| eval overdue=if(now()>strptime(review_date,"%Y-%m-%d"),1,0)
| stats latest(residual_score) as score, max(overdue) as past_due by risk_id, title
| where past_due=1 OR score>15
| sort score
```

Understanding this SPL

**Formal Risk Assessment Evidence and Residual Risk Score Trend (PCI DSS Req 12.3.1, 12.3.2)** — Tracks annual enterprise risk assessment submissions including PCI-scoped risk items and residual scores so targeted risk analyses required by the standard are current and leadership-reviewed.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=grc` `sourcetype="grc:risk_register"` — `risk_id`, `pci_relevant`, `residual_score`, `review_date`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk DB Connect or `sourcetype="grc:risk_register"`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: grc; **sourcetype**: grc:risk_register. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=grc, sourcetype="grc:risk_register", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where pci_relevant="true"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **overdue** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by risk_id, title** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• Filters the current rows with `where past_due=1 OR score>15` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line (residual_score trend in summary index), table (past_due risks), heatmap (domain x score).
grandmaExplanation: We watch for tracks annual enterprise risk assessment submissions including pci-scoped risk items and residual scores so targeted risk analyses required by the standard are current and leadership-reviewed. This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
